Title: Discovery of FoTO1 and Taxol genes enables biosynthesis of baccatin III

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FoTO1 should be included when reconstructing early paclitaxel/taxane pathways because it strongly improves productive first oxidation and suppresses derailment products; useful for pathway stacking with TDS/T5αH/T13αH in heterologous hosts. Likely a key enabling factor rather than a standalone trait gene. Caution: homologues from Arabidopsis and a Taxus paralogue did not substitute, so orthologue choice is critical.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes productive first taxane oxidation, increases on-pathway taxane intermediates 10–17-fold, suppresses major rearranged side products, and enables higher baccatin III/advanced taxane production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with pathway genes; GC-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; truncation/mutagenesis; microscale thermophoresis binding assays; co-immunoprecipitation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Taxol module 1 and related pathway genes were activated by elicitation, especially methyl jasmonate/chitosan-related perturbation conditions in Taxus needles; heterologous validations were in N. benthamiana leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Taxus, FoTO1 is highly ranked in Taxol module 1, co-expressed with early Taxol genes and induced in elicited biosynthetic cell states rather than naive mature tissues.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Taxus media var. hicksii, co-expressed with early Taxol pathway under elicited states.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not reported; described as module 1 rank 13 candidate
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn in Taxus media; bulk RNA-seq comparison datasets from PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FoTO1 is an NTF2-like protein not previously implicated in plant specialized metabolism. It is not a catalytic taxane synthase itself; instead likely acts as a scaffold/facilitator. C-terminal helix is required for in planta function and protein binding. It also streamlines T13αH oxidation of taxadiene.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth penalty or yield penalty reported in heterologous host; dependence on correct species-specific FoTO1 suggests portability limits.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NTF2-like protein with critical C-terminal alpha-helix
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FoTO1 is a newly discovered NTF2-like factor essential for efficient early paclitaxel pathway reconstitution. 2. It binds T5αH and TDS2 and suppresses side-product formation during the first oxidation. 3. Inclusion of FoTO1 boosts pathway output and enables productive flux toward baccatin III and later Taxol intermediates.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Paclitaxel precursor used for anticancer chemotherapy; taxanes function in plant chemical defense

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use T9αH-750C rather than T9αH-725A for baccatin III/Taxol-branch reconstitution because its substrate specificity fits the non-13-acetoxy route. Valuable for heterologous paclitaxel precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxane intermediate 4 at C-9α, leading to 4O3A intermediate and, with downstream acetylation, taxusin-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables progression from early oxidized taxane 4 toward Taxol-branch intermediates; depletes 3O2A and forms 4O3A/4O4A products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolite profiling; NMR/MS-MS structural confirmation of downstream taxusin products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an intermediate precursor on the main pathway to baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Discovered from elicited Taxus module 1 expression states; function validated in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly ranked in Taxol module 1, associated with early Taxol genes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In elicited Taxus media biosynthetic states associated with early pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to CYP750C family, not CYP725A. Represents independently evolved T9αH activity distinct from previously reported T9αH-725A, which appears to prefer 13α-acetoxy substrates and is incompatible with the baccatin III route used here.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core Taxol branch using C-13α-hydroxyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alternative T9αH-725A cannot substitute in this reconstruction; enzyme choice strongly affects pathway flux and branch specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4O3A taxane intermediate; downstream taxusin-related products with acetyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important branch-defining step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Not reported; inferred ER membrane typical of P450s but not directly stated for this enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T9αH-750C is a newly identified C-9α taxane hydroxylase from module 1. 2. It functions on the non-13-acetoxy Taxol branch and is required for productive baccatin III reconstitution. 3. It is evolutionarily distinct from CYP725A T9αHs, showing convergent evolution and substrate specialization.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: T7AT should be stacked with TOT and upstream genes because 7β-acylation is required for efficient oxetane/epoxide-forming TOT activity. Essential in engineering the baccatin III route.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers acyl groups, including acetyl, to the taxane C-7β hydroxyl.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates 7β-O-acylated intermediates that permit TOT-mediated conversion to hepta-oxidized products on the route to baccatin VI/baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate acyltransferase screening in N. benthamiana; LC-MS pathway reconstruction; product depletion/formation with and without TOT.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product intermediate and enables downstream oxidation/oxetane formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from Taxol module 2; validated in N. benthamiana pathway reconstructions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ranked 15 in Taxol module 2, associated with later pathway expression states.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Taxus module 2 biosynthetic states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalyses various C-7β-O-acylations, including acetylation. Although independently reported elsewhere, this study established its importance for TOT function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Adds a transient acetyl group absent from final baccatin III, requiring later deacetylation by T7dA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enabling step for TOT function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T7AT acylates the C-7β hydroxyl of mid-pathway taxanes. 2. This cryptic acylation is required for efficient TOT activity. 3. It reveals a protection/deprotection strategy in paclitaxel precursor biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: T1βH-686 is preferred over T1βH-184 for engineering because it yields significantly higher levels of desired 2α-benzoylated downstream product 10-Bz. Best choice for baccatin III pathway assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase that hydroxylates taxane intermediates at C-1β; can also generate rearranged abeo-taxane byproducts depending on substrate context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Installs the previously missing C-1β hydroxyl needed for advanced oxygenated taxanes and baccatin VI/baccatin III route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Oxidase screening in N. benthamiana with multiple upstream pathways; LC-MS/MS product detection; downstream baccatin VI formation with TAX19-supported pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor functionality required for baccatin VI/baccatin III synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from Taxol module 2 and validated in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in later Taxol module 2 biosynthetic states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two paralogous T1βHs discovered. Shows better performance for desired benzoylated branch than T1βH-184.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidation branch toward baccatin VI/baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can generate multiple mono-oxidized products; off-pathway rearrangements possible depending on substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 1β-hydroxylated highly oxygenated taxanes leading to baccatin VI (11-Bz)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing late oxidation step; not explicitly stated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T1βH-686 is a newly identified 2-ODD that performs the long-sought C-1β hydroxylation. 2. It supports formation of baccatin VI and downstream baccatin III. 3. It is the preferred engineering choice over T1βH-184 due to higher desired product levels.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative T1βH capable of the missing C-1β hydroxylation, but less preferred than T1βH-686 for productive benzoylated branch engineering due to lower desired 10-Bz levels and more byproduct complexity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase that hydroxylates taxanes at C-1β and can also promote rearrangement to 11(15→1)abeo-taxanes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports missing C-1β hydroxylation but also yields rearranged abeo-taxane products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-expression; LC-MS; purification and NMR characterization of 1β-hydroxytaxusin and 15-hydroxy-11(15→1)abeo-taxusin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor functionality, but also diverts flux toward side-route products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified in Taxol module 2; validated in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in module 2 later pathway states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrated mechanistic basis for radical rearrangement to non-classical abeo-taxanes. Useful for understanding pathway branching and enzyme promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidation branch and abeo-taxane side-route
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greater side-product formation and less desirable flux to the benzoylated Taxol branch than T1βH-686.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Taxusin and highly oxygenated mid/late intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing late oxidation step; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T1βH-184 performs C-1β hydroxylation of taxanes. 2. It also forms rearranged abeo-taxanes, revealing competing chemistry during pathway evolution. 3. It confirms the identity of the previously missing 1β-hydroxylase class but is less optimal for engineering than T1βH-686.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Include T9dA downstream of cryptic acetylation steps to remove the transient C-9 acetyl and enable T9ox action; essential for successful baccatin III reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes/removes the C-9 O-acetyl group from advanced taxanes such as baccatin VI-related intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Permits downstream C-9 oxidation and conversion toward baccatin III; prerequisite for T9ox function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Feeding assays with baccatin VI/9DHAB in N. benthamiana; stepwise pathway assembly; LC-MS/MS analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for deprotection/modification of target-product intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from modules 2/3 candidate α/β-hydrolases; validated in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Module-associated candidate from later Taxol pathway states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally identified as DeAc898; renamed after functional assignment. Demonstrates a protection/deprotection logic in paclitaxel precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late deacetylation module toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Necessary removal of transient protective acetyl may expose intermediates to instability; no growth penalties reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enabling step for downstream T9ox
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T9dA removes the cryptic C-9 acetyl introduced earlier in the pathway. 2. This deacetylation is required before T9ox can oxidize C-9 to the ketone. 3. It is essential for reconstitution of baccatin III biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct paclitaxel precursor used in semisynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stack T7dA with T9dA and T9ox to complete late-stage deprotection and maximize baccatin III formation from transiently acetylated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes/removes the C-7β O-acetyl group from advanced taxane intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Removes transient protective C-7 acetyl group to furnish deprotected late intermediates on route to baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Feeding assays and full pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with standards.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for deprotection/modification of target-product intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from modules 2/3 α/β-hydrolase candidates; validated in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Module-associated candidate from later Taxol pathway states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally DeAc1023; later renamed T7dA. Works with T9dA to strip two transient acetates absent from final baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late deacetylation module toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reflects need for extra pathway length and deprotection steps in engineering designs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T7dA removes the transient C-7β acetyl used earlier to enable TOT chemistry. 2. Together with T9dA, it converts over-acetylated late intermediates toward the final baccatin III scaffold. 3. It clarifies a protection/deprotection strategy in Taxol precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct paclitaxel precursor used in semisynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential downstream oxidation step for baccatin III production; should be stacked after T9dA/T7dA. Important for semi-synthesis precursor production platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes taxane C-9 alcohol-derived intermediate to a ketone, likely with loss of two hydrogens, converting 9DHAB-like substrate toward baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the C-9 ketone required for final baccatin III scaffold; enables abundant baccatin III formation with deacetylases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate oxidase feeding assay with 9DHAB; full pathway reconstitution; NMR and MS/MS confirmation of baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or immediate penultimate structure
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from later Taxol modules and validated in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Taxus later biosynthetic states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A 2-ODD family oxidase; independently reported by another group. In dropout experiments, could be omitted with only partial loss, indicating some functional compensation in heterologous context, but it is part of optimal pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal ketone-forming branch toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Late oxidation depends on prior deacetylation; omission reduces but may not fully abolish baccatin III under this system.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Baccatin III or immediate ketone-containing precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for C-9 ketone formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T9ox catalyses the missing C-9 oxidation needed for baccatin III. 2. Together with T9dA and T7dA, it completes the updated late pathway. 3. Its inclusion enabled NMR-confirmed de novo biosynthesis of baccatin III in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana using pEAQ-HT; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Functional PCL is required for installing the paclitaxel side chain in heterologous systems. AAE-867.5 should replace previously proposed PCL candidates that failed in this host context.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates beta-phenylalanine as a CoA thioester, supplying the side-chain donor used by BAPT for acylation of baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel when co-expressed with PAM and BAPT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate AAE screening by co-expression with PAM and BAPT in N. benthamiana with fed or de novo produced baccatin III; LC-MS detection of product 17.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies activated side-chain precursor for target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified from Taxol expression modules; function validated in N. benthamiana with fed-in baccatin III and full pathway background.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Single prominent AAE candidate in Taxol modules.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Taxus module-associated side-chain biosynthetic states.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn; bulk RNA-seq comparison PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported AAEs proposed as PCL did not function in this system; AAE-867.5 was the productive PCL candidate. Enabled first de novo biosynthesis of 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Full Taxol was still not achieved because T2'αH activity could not be reconstituted; PCL alone is insufficient for final product completion.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for side-chain installation in this system
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAE-867.5 is the functional beta-phenylalanine-CoA ligase required for paclitaxel side-chain biosynthesis. 2. It works with PAM and BAPT to form 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel from baccatin III. 3. It resolved a major remaining gap in late Taxol pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; native Taxus tissues implied
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Late paclitaxel precursor with anticancer pharmaceutical relevance
